label: Lyft
description: Connect Lyft to request rides, read ride history, and manage Lyft Business accounts.

form.title: Connect Lyft
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your Lyft rides and account via your Lyft developer app.

methods.label: Sign in with Lyft (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: Opens Lyft's consent screen in your browser so Anton can request and read rides on your behalf. You'll need a Lyft developer app (Client ID + Client Secret) from the Lyft Developer Portal — takes about 5 minutes to set up.
methods.how_to:
## Sign in with Lyft (OAuth 2.0 — Bring Your Own App)

Anton doesn't ship a hosted Lyft OAuth client, so you'll register a free developer app first. It takes about 5 minutes.

### 1. Create a Lyft Developer App

1. Go to [www.lyft.com/developers](https://www.lyft.com/developers) and sign in with your Lyft account.
2. Click **Create new app**.
3. Fill in an app name (e.g. `Anton`) and description. The redirect URI must be `http://localhost` or a loopback address — Anton handles this automatically.
4. Click **Save**. You'll land on the app's detail page.

### 2. Copy Your Credentials

5. On the app detail page, copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** into the fields below.

### 3. Authorize

6. Click **Submit**. Anton opens Lyft's consent screen in your browser.
7. Log in and approve the requested scopes:
   - `public` — public Lyft data
   - `profile` — your Lyft profile
   - `rides.request` — request rides on your behalf
   - `rides.read` — view your ride history
8. After approving, the browser redirects back and Anton stores your access and refresh tokens locally.

### Notes

- The Client Secret is sensitive — anyone with it can act as your app. Keep it out of shared documents.
- If your token expires or is revoked, re-run this flow to re-authorize.
- For Lyft Business accounts, make sure you sign in with the account that has business permissions.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: From the Lyft Developer Portal — app detail page → Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID. Found on the same app detail page in the Lyft Developer Portal.
